book: Class 07 - English - An Alien Hand - Revised

questions.question_text:
The story of an ant's life sounds almost untrue.
The italicised phrase means
highly exaggerated.
too remarkable to be true.
not based on facts.
questions.question_html:
The story of an ant’s life sounds almost untrue.
The italicised phrase means

  1. highly exaggerated.
  2. too remarkable to be true.
  3. not based on facts.

questions.question_text:
Complete the following sentences.
An ant is the smallest, ___________
We know a number of facts about an ant's life because __________
questions.question_html:
Complete the following sentences.

  1. An ant is the smallest, ___________
  2. We know a number of facts about an ant’s life because __________

solutions.text:
An ant is the smallest, the commonest and the wisest insect.
We know a number of facts about an ant's life because people have kept ants as pets and have watched their daily behaviour closely.
solutions.html:
1. An ant is the smallest, the commonest and the wisest insect.
  2. We know a number of facts about an ant’s life because people have kept ants as pets and have watched their daily behaviour closely.

questions.question_text: In what ways is an ant's life peaceful? [The Tiny Teacher]
questions.question_html: In what ways is an ant’s life peaceful? [The Tiny Teacher]

solutions.text: An ant's life is peaceful because each does its work intelligently and bravely and never fights with other members of its group. Each member has a designated place to stay and a definite work to do. a soldier ant never goes out to search for food and similarly, a worker never enters the domain of a soldier ant. No worker or soldier has ever harmed any grub. Each one loyally and diligently does his duty and relentlessly carries on with their work peacefully. They know sharing and contribution and do not interfere in each other's work.
solutions.html: An ant’s life is peaceful because each does its work intelligently and bravely and never fights with other members of its group. Each member has a designated place to stay and a definite work to do. a soldier ant never goes out to search for food and similarly, a worker never enters the domain of a soldier ant. No worker or soldier has ever harmed any grub. Each one loyally and diligently does his duty and relentlessly carries on with their work peacefully. They know sharing and contribution and do not interfere in each other’s work.

questions.question_text: How long does it take for a grub to become a complete ant?
questions.question_html: How long does it take for a grub to become a complete ant?

solutions.text: It takes two or three weeks for a grub to become a cocoon. After another three weeks, the cocoon breaks and a complete ant appears.
solutions.html: It takes two or three weeks for a grub to become a cocoon. After another three weeks, the cocoon breaks and a complete ant appears.

questions.question_text: Why do the worker ants carry the grubs about?
questions.question_html: Why do the worker ants carry the grubs about?

solutions.text: The worker ants carry them about daily for airing, exercise, and sunshine.
solutions.html: The worker ants carry them about daily for airing, exercise, and sunshine.

questions.question_text: What jobs are new ants trained for?
questions.question_html: What jobs are new ants trained for?

solutions.text: The new ants are trained as workers, soldiers, builders, cleaners, etc.
solutions.html: The new ants are trained as workers, soldiers, builders, cleaners, etc.

questions.question_text: Name some other creatures that live in anthills. Answer in the context of The Tiny Teacher.
questions.question_html: Name some other creatures that live in anthills. Answer in the context of The Tiny Teacher.

solutions.text: Some other creatures that live in anthills are beetles, lesser breeds of ants, and the greenfly.
solutions.html: Some other creatures that live in anthills are beetles, lesser breeds of ants, and the greenfly.

questions.question_text: Mention three things we can learn from the 'tiny teacher'. Give reasons for choosing these items.
questions.question_html: Mention three things we can learn from the ‘tiny teacher’. Give reasons for choosing these items.

solutions.text:
Though ants are very small and unassertive they are undoubtedly great teachers for our life. We can learn teamwork as ants do their work by sharing and contributing without interfering in each other's work.
We can learn hard work as ants spend their most of time in doing their respective jobs without hesitation.
We can learn loyalty, power of cohesive work and discipline as ants live a disciplined life and always follow the rules of their group and are loyal towards it.
solutions.html:
Though ants are very small and unassertive they are undoubtedly great teachers for our life. We can learn teamwork as ants do their work by sharing and contributing without interfering in each other's work.
We can learn hard work as ants spend their most of time in doing their respective jobs without hesitation.
We can learn loyalty, power of cohesive work and discipline as ants live a disciplined life and always follow the rules of their group and are loyal towards it.

questions.question_text:
What problems are you likely to face if you keep ants as pets?
When a group of bees finds nectar, it informs other bees of its location, quantity, etc. through dancing. Can you guess what ants communicate to their fellow ants by touching one another's feelers?
questions.question_html:
1. What problems are you likely to face if you keep ants as pets?
  2. When a group of bees finds nectar, it informs other bees of its location, quantity, etc. through dancing. Can you guess what ants communicate to their fellow ants by touching one another’s feelers?

solutions.text:
Keeping ants as pets is very uncommon or rare. The reason is that they don't take orders. They follow their own rules. Above all, they bite and spoil the food.
By touching one another's feelers, the ants give a message to others about new-found food.
solutions.html:
1. Keeping ants as pets is very uncommon or rare. The reason is that they don't take orders. They follow their own rules. Above all, they bite and spoil the food.
  2. By touching one another's feelers, the ants give a message to others about new-found food.

questions.question_text:
Complete the following poem with words from the box below. Then recite the poem.
Soldiers live in barracks
And birds in ________.
Much like a snake that rests
In a ________. No horse is able
To sleep except in a ________
And a dog lives well,
Mind you, only in a ________.
To say 'hi' to an ant, if you will,
You may have to climb an ________.


hole, kennel, nests, anthill, stable
